texto: In the beginning of all things, wisdom and knowledge were with the animals, for Tirawa, the One Above, did not speak directly to man. He sent certain animals to tell men that he showed himself through the beast, and that from them, and from the stars and the sun and moon should man learn.. all things tell of Tirawa. What is life? It is the flash of a firefly in the night. It is the breath of a buffalo in the wintertime. It is the little shadow which runs across the grass and loses itself in the sunset.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Eagle Chief Letakos-Lesa Pawnee que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “In the beginning of all things, wisdom and knowledge were with the animals, for Tirawa, the One A…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, animals, god, com foco em boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
